title: Qualia
description: What it's like - experiential knowledge, pain points, solutions

triple-export-registration-pain.title: Triple export registration easy to forget
triple-export-registration-pain.description: Adding new repository requires updating 3 separate locations in index.ts, easy to miss one
triple-export-registration-pain.impact: Causes 'Module has no exported member' errors at typecheck
triple-export-registration-pain.resolution: Follow 9-step entity checklist, always update all 3 locations

stale-dist-folders-pain.title: Stale dist folders cause false typecheck errors
stale-dist-folders-pain.description: TypeScript composite projects cache build artifacts that can become stale
stale-dist-folders-pain.impact: Typecheck fails even with correct code, frustrating debugging
stale-dist-folders-pain.frequency: after major changes or branch switches

tsv-no-headers.title: TSV exports have no headers
tsv-no-headers.description: MoneyWorks TSV format doesn't include field names, making parsing difficult without prior knowledge
tsv-no-headers.impact: Must use XML exports to discover field structure before parsing TSV

client-recreation-overhead.title: Client recreation overhead
client-recreation-overhead.description: Creating new MoneyWorks clients for each request is expensive
client-recreation-overhead.impact: Performance degradation with high request volume

dev-server-memory-pressure.title: Dev server crashes under memory pressure
dev-server-memory-pressure.description: React Router dev server crashes with exit code 137 (SIGKILL) when system memory is constrained by multiple Bun processes
dev-server-memory-pressure.impact: Blocks browser testing for UI validation, requires static validation workarounds
dev-server-memory-pressure.resolution: Use static validation (TypeScript compilation, API testing, grep-based validation) instead of browser testing
evidence: AC-026 and AC-027 blocked by exit code 137

xml-field-discovery.title: XML-based Field Discovery
xml-field-discovery.description: Use XML exports (which include field names) to discover table structure, then use efficient TSV for data

client-caching-with-ttl.title: LRU Client Caching
client-caching-with-ttl.description: Cache clients by token with 10-minute TTL and periodic cleanup

static-validation-for-blocked-tests.title: Static Validation as Testing Fallback
static-validation-for-blocked-tests.description: When browser testing is blocked by environment, use TypeScript compilation, API endpoint checks, and grep-based validation to verify implementation correctness
tradeoffs: Can verify routes registered, types compile, API endpoints available
evidence: US-002: 25/27 ACs validated via static analysis, 2 blocked by environment

export-workflow.name: MoneyWorks Data Export
export-workflow.description: Complete workflow for exporting MoneyWorks data in multiple formats

full-stack-entity-workflow.name: Complete Entity Implementation
full-stack-entity-workflow.description: Autonomous workflow for implementing full-stack MoneyWorks entity from canonical types through UI
steps: Phase 1: Canonical Layer (enums → types → fields → validators → index)
Phase 2: Data Layer (repository + specialized queries + tests)
Phase 4: UI Layer (routes + navigation + list/detail views)
Phase 5: Integration Testing (static validation + browser testing)
full-stack-entity-workflow.automation: fully automated via Task tool + specialized agents
full-stack-entity-workflow.parallelization: Independent tasks run in parallel (e.g., fields + validators, list route + detail route)
evidence: US-002: 25 tasks completed autonomously in ~1.5 hours

reference-implementation-pattern.title: Use existing entity as reference implementation
reference-implementation-pattern.description: When implementing new entity, copy structure from existing entity (e.g., Product for Account)
reference-implementation-pattern.rationale: Ensures consistency, reduces cognitive load, establishes baseline structure
examples: ACCT-001 used Product entity as reference for Account implementation
Copy enums.ts structure, types.ts interface format, repository method categories

domain-specific-query-methods.title: Add domain-specific query methods beyond requirements
domain-specific-query-methods.description: Include natural complements to required methods that address common use cases
domain-specific-query-methods.rationale: Anticipates developer needs, provides complete API without scope creep
examples: findBankAccounts() for accounts with banking info

preserve-canonical-dsl.title: Always Preserve MoneyWorks Terminology
preserve-canonical-dsl.description: NEVER translate MoneyWorks field names - use GST not VAT, TaxCode not taxId, etc.
preserve-canonical-dsl.rationale: Maintains semantic integrity and API consistency across all MoneyWorks installations globally
examples: Use 'GST' for all tax types (even US sales tax or EU VAT)

use-branded-types.title: Use Branded Types for Domain Primitives
use-branded-types.description: Wrap domain primitives in branded types to prevent misuse
use-branded-types.rationale: Compile-time prevention of passing wrong types (e.g., ISO date where YYYYMMDD expected)

multi-format-support.title: Support Multiple Export Formats
multi-format-support.description: Provide compact (arrays), compact-headers, full (objects), and schema (enriched) formats
multi-format-support.rationale: Different use cases need different trade-offs between bandwidth, DX, and metadata

cache-clients.title: Cache MoneyWorks Clients
cache-clients.description: Cache clients by token with TTL to avoid recreation overhead
cache-clients.rationale: Creating new MoneyWorks clients for each request is expensive

parallel-task-execution.title: Execute Independent Tasks in Parallel
parallel-task-execution.description: When using Task tool, identify independent tasks and execute in parallel to reduce total time
parallel-task-execution.rationale: Parallel execution reduces total story time by ~30%
examples: T-028 (list route) + T-029 (detail route) in parallel
evidence: US-002: Reduced execution time from ~2 hours to ~1.5 hours

evidence-based-ac-validation.title: Collect Concrete Evidence for Each AC
evidence-based-ac-validation.description: For every acceptance criterion, collect specific evidence: file paths, line numbers, test output, API responses
evidence-based-ac-validation.rationale: Objective validation provides clear audit trail and prevents false positives
examples: AC-012: TypeScript compilation zero errors (bun run tsc --noEmit output)
evidence: US-002: Created comprehensive validation report with evidence for all 27 ACs
